description: This schematic diagram illustrates the distribution of different deformation zones within a cylindrical compression sample of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density high-strength steel after hot deformation. The figure presents a cross-sectional view parallel to the loading direction (indicated by loading force P), revealing three distinct zones marked as 1, 2, and 3. Zone 1 (difficult deformation zone) appears at the top and bottom center regions near the contact surfaces with the compression platens; Zone 2 (small deformation zone) is located at the peripheral edges; and Zone 3 (large deformation zone) occupies the central and transitional areas. The non-uniform deformation pattern arises from friction between the sample contact surfaces and the compression platens, leading to uneven stress distribution during isothermal constant strain rate compression (conducted at 900–1150°C with 60% reduction). This schematic establishes the spatial framework for subsequent microstructural characterization (referenced as Fig. 9 in the source text), where the dynamic recrystallization (DRX) behavior varies significantly across these zones—Zone 3 exhibiting the most substantial DRX while Zone 1 shows minimal deformation. The diagram serves as a critical reference for understanding how processing parameters and friction conditions induce heterogeneous microstructure evolution in the austenitic steel matrix.
